fault_hypothesis: The `overlaps` function on line 64 uses strict inequalities (`b[1] <= a[0]` and `b[0] >= a[1]`), which treats adjacent windows (where one ends exactly where the other begins) as non-overlapping. The failing tests expect adjacent boundaries to be treated as overlapping, so the condition should use strict less-than (`b[1] < a[0]` and `b[0] > a[1]`).

spec.expr: not (candidate[1] < existing[0][0] or candidate[0] > existing[0][1]) == True

constraints.intent: Check that adjacent windows (touching boundaries) are correctly identified as overlapping at the overlaps definition line.

spec.expr: not (candidate[1] == slot[0] or candidate[0] == slot[1]) or not (slot[1] <= candidate[0] or slot[0] >= candidate[1])

constraints.intent: At each loop iteration, verify that when candidate touches a slot boundary, the overlaps function returns True (i.e., does not treat them as non-overlapping).

spec.expr: all(merged__1[i][1] <= merged__1[i+1][0] for i in range(len(merged__1)-1))

constraints.intent: After building merged, verify no two windows in the result share or overlap boundaries, which would indicate the overlap check was too permissive.
When the overlaps branch is taken, ensure it also fires for boundary-touching cases (this will be False when adjacent windows incorrectly pass through).
